title: An automated storage and retrieval system comprising a temperature management system and a method for managing temperature in the automated storage and retrieval system
abstract: A temperature management system for the storage volume of an automated storage and retrieval system. The temperature management system comprises a chilled air plant, and a plurality of pairs of oppositely arranged, horizontally extending air ducts being disposed in the framework volume, said plurality of pairs of air ducts being for conveying air at a first and a second temperature to the framework volume. Also disclosed is a method for managing air in an automated, grid-based storage and retrieval system.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS i. An automated, grid-based storage and retrieval system (i), the system (i) comprising: - a framework structure (100) comprising vertically extending members (102) defining a framework volume (104), said framework volume (104) being thermally isolated, and a network of horizontal rails (110, 111) provided at upper ends of said vertical members (102), wherein the network is configured to support vehicles for handling goods holders (106) , - wherein the framework volume (104) comprises a storage volume (500) with vertically extending storage columns (105) for storing goods holders (106), said storage volume (500) being disposed below the horizontal rails (110, 111) and at a distance from said horizontal rails (110, 111), and - a temperature management system (20) for the storage volume (500) comprising: - a chilled air plant (22), - a first pair of oppositely arranged, horizontally extending air ducts (23a, 23b) being disposed in the framework volume (104), said first pair of air ducts (23a, 23b) being for conveying air at a first temperature to the framework volume (104), wherein the first pair of air ducts (23a, 23b) are each provided with a first air release device configured to release the air at the first temperature into the framework volume (104) in a horizontal direction, and wherein the first air release devices of the respective first pair of air ducts (23a, 23b) face one another, - a second pair of oppositely arranged, horizontally extending air ducts (24a, 24b) being disposed in the framework volume (104) and below said first pair of air ducts (23a, 23b), said second pair of air ducts (24a, 24b) being for conveying air at a second temperature to the framework volume (104), wherein the second pair of air ducts (24a, 24b) are each provided with a second air release device configured to release the air at the second temperature into the framework volume (104) in a horizontal direction, and wherein the second air release devices of the respective second pair of air ducts (24a, 24b) face one another, 

 - a third pair of oppositely arranged, horizontally extending air ducts (25a, 25b) being disposed in the framework volume (104) and below said second pair of air ducts (24a, 24b), said third pair of air ducts being for conveying air at the second temperature to the framework volume (104), wherein the third pair of air ducts (25a, 25b) are each provided with a third air release device configured to release the air at the second temperature downwards into the framework volume (104). 2. A system (1) of claim 1, wherein the first pair of air ducts (23a, 23b) are configured to release air at the first temperature into a first region (31) of the framework volume (104), said first region (31) being disposed below the horizontal rails (110, 111) and above the storage volume (500). 3. A system (1) of claim 2, wherein the second pair of air ducts (24a, 24b) are configured to release air at the second temperature into a second region (32) of the framework volume (104), said second region (32) being disposed below the first region (31) and above the storage volume (500). 4. A system (1) of any of the preceding claims, wherein the first air temperature is above o Â°C and the second air temperature is below o Â°C. 5. A system (1) of any of the preceding claims, wherein the temperature management system (20) comprises: - a pair of oppositely arranged, horizontally extending return air ducts (27a, 27b) being disposed in the framework volume (104) and below said third pair of air ducts (25a, 25b),) and at the bottom of the storage grid (104), wherein the return air ducts (24a, 24b) are for extracting air from the storage volume (500) such that the air is conveyed towards the chilled air plant (22). 6. A system (1) of claim 5, wherein a section of a floor supporting the framework structure (100) makes up a bottom side of the return air duct (27a, 27b) and lowermost section of the air duct sides adjacent said bottom side has an indented pattern such that air of the storage volume may enter said return air duct (27a, 27b). 7. A system (1) of any of the preceding claims, wherein the air ducts (23a-25b, 27a-2yb) extend in a first (X) direction of the framework structure (100) at least for a majority of a distance between two opposite sides of said framework structure (100). 8. A system (1) of any of the preceding claims, wherein at least one of the first, the second and the third air release devices has openings arranged in a rectangular pattern. 9. A system (1) of claim 8, wherein each of the first, the second and the third air release devices comprises a plurality of equidistantly arranged air release devices extending along a straight line. 10. A system (1) of claim 9, wherein a distance between two adjacent first air release devices is the same as a distance between two adjacent second air release devices and is smaller than a distance between two adjacent third air release devices. 11. A system (1) of any of the preceding claims, wherein a first pair of air conduits (51a, 51b) connects interior of the chilled air plant (22) and the second pair of oppositely arranged, horizontally extending air ducts (24a, 24b) such that air at the second temperature flows from the interior of the chilled air plant (22) to said second pair of air ducts (24a, 24b). 12. A system (1) of claim 11, wherein each conduit of the first pair of air conduits (51a, 51b) comprises a damper and a fan. 13. A system (1) of any of the preceding claims, wherein a second pair of air conduits (52a, 52b) connects an interior of the chilled air plant (22) and the third pair of oppositely arranged, horizontally extending air ducts (25a, 25b) such that air at the second temperature flows from the interior of the temperature-control unit (22) to said third pair of air ducts (25a, 25b). 14. A system (1) of claim 13, wherein each conduit of the second pair of air conduits (52a, 52b) comprises a damper and a fan. 15. A system (1) of any of the claims 5-14, wherein a third pair of air conduits (53a, 53b) connects the return air ducts (27a, 27b) and an interior of the chilled air plant (22) such that air flows from each air duct (27, 27b) to the interior of the chilled air plant (22). 16. A system (1) of claim 15, wherein each conduit of the third pair of air conduits (53a, 53b) comprises a damper and a fan. 17- A system (1) of any of the claims 15-16, wherein a fourth pair of air conduits (54a, 54b) connects each of the return air ducts (27a, 27b) to a respective air conduit (51a, 51b) of the first pair of air conduits (51a, 51b). 18. A system (1) of claim 17, wherein each conduit of the fourth pair of air conduits (54a, 54b) comprises a damper and a fan. 19. A system (1) of any of the preceding claims, wherein the chilled air plant (22) comprises an external air dehumidifier, and a fifth air conduit (55a) connects an interior of the chilled air plant (22) with the external air dehumidifier such that air flows from the interior of the temperature-control unit (22) to the external dehumidifier thereby reducing its humidity, and wherein a sixth air conduit (56a) is configured to connect the external air dehumidifier with the first pair of oppositely arranged, horizontally extending air ducts (23a, 23b) such that air with reduced humidity flows from the external air dehumidifier to said first pair of air ducts (24a, 24b). 20. A system (1) of claim 19, wherein the fifth air conduit (55a) comprises a damper. 21. A system (1) of claim 19 or claim 20, wherein a heat battery is associated with the sixth air conduit (56a). 22. A system (1) of any of the claims 19-21, wherein a seventh air conduit (57a) connects the sixth air conduit (56a) with the interior of the chilled air plant (22) such that air flows from the sixth air conduit (56a) to the interior of the chilled air plant (22). 23. A system (1) of claim 22, wherein the seventh air conduit (57a) comprises a damper. 24. A system (1) of any of the preceding claims, wherein a damper is provided in the thermally isolating structure surrounding the framework volume (104). 25. A system (1) of claim 24, wherein said damper enables fluid communication between a horizontally extending air duct of the second pair of air ducts (24a, 24b) and an exterior of the framework volume (104). 26. A method for managing temperature in an automated, grid-based storage and retrieval system (1) comprising a framework structure (100) comprising vertically extending members (102) defining a framework volume (104), said 

framework volume (104) being thermally isolated, and a network of horizontal rails (110, 111) provided at upper ends of said vertical members (102), wherein remotely operated vehicles for handling goods holders (106) operate on top of said network, the framework volume (104) comprising a storage volume (500) with vertically extending storage columns (105) for storing goods holders (106), said storage volume (500) being disposed below the horizontal rails (110, 111) and at a distance from said horizontal rails (110, 111), said system comprising a temperature management system (20) for the storage volume (500) comprising a chilled air plant (22), the method comprising: - conveying air at a first temperature to the framework volume (104) using a pair of oppositely arranged first air ducts (23a, 23b), - releasing air at the first temperature into the framework volume (14) in a horizontal direction such that two oppositely directed air streams are created, - conveying air at a second temperature to the framework volume (104) using a pair of oppositely arranged second air ducts (24a, 24b), - releasing air at the second temperature into the framework volume (104) in a horizontal direction such that two oppositely directed air streams are created. - conveying air at the second temperature to the framework volume (104) using a pair of oppositely arranged third air ducts (25a, 25b), - releasing air at the first temperature downwards into the framework volume (104). 27. A method of claim 26, wherein the first air temperature is above o Â°C and the second air temperature is below o Â°C. 28. A method of any of the claims 26-27, said method comprising: - releasing air at the first temperature in the horizontal direction in a first region (31) disposed below the horizontal rails (110, 111) and above the storage volume (500), - releasing air at the second temperature in the horizontal direction in a second region (32) disposed below the first region (31) and above the storage volume (500). 29. A method of any of the claims 26-28, said method comprising: 

 - extracting air from the storage volume (500) via return air ducts (27a, 27b) disposed in the framework volume (104) below said third pair of air ducts (25a, 25b), and - conveying the extracted air to an interior of the chilled air plant (22). 30. A method of claim 29, said method comprising: - preventing the extracted air from entering the interior of the chilled air plant (22) while said interior of the chilled air plant (22) is being defrosted. 31. A method of claim 30, said method comprising: - discharging air from the horizontally extending air duct of the second pair of air ducts (24a, 24b) to an exterior of the framework volume (104) while the interior of the chilled air plant (22) is being defrosted, 32. A method of any of the claims 28-31, wherein, prior to the conveying of the air at the first temperature using the first air ducts (23a, 23b), said method comprises: - adjusting air temperature so that it reaches said first temperature, and/or - dehumidifying the air so that it reaches a set humidity level. 33. A temperature management system (20) for a storage volume (500) of an automated, grid-based storage and retrieval system (1) comprising a framework structure (100) comprising vertically extending members (102) defining a thermally isolated framework volume (104), said storage volume (500) being part of the framework volume (104) of the system (1), the temperature management system (20) comprising: - a chilled air plant (22), - a first pair of oppositely arranged, horizontally extending air ducts (23a, 23b) configured to be disposed in the framework volume (104), said first pair of air ducts (23a, 23b) being for conveying air at a first temperature to the framework volume (104), wherein the first pair of air ducts (23a, 23b) are each provided with a first air release device configured to release the air at the first temperature into the framework volume (104) in a horizontal direction, and 

 wherein the first air release devices of the respective first pair of air ducts (23a, 23b) face one another, - a second pair of oppositely arranged, horizontally extending air ducts (24a, 24b) configured to be disposed in the framework volume (104) and below said first pair of air ducts (23a, 23b), said second pair of air ducts (24a, 24b) being for conveying air at a second temperature to the framework volume (104), wherein the second pair of air ducts (24a, 24b) are each provided with a second air release device configured to release the air at the second temperature into the framework volume (104) in a horizontal direction, and wherein the second air release devices of the respective second pair of air ducts (24a, 24b) face one another, - a third pair of oppositely arranged, horizontally extending air ducts (25a, 25b) configured to be disposed in the framework volume (104) and below said second pair of air ducts (24a, 24b), said third pair of air ducts being for conveying air at the second temperature to the framework volume (104), wherein the third pair of air ducts (25a, 25b) are each provided with a third air release device configured to release the air at the second temperature downwards into the framework volume (104), - a plurality of air conduits (5ia-52b; 54a-57a) connecting the chilled air plant (22) with the air ducts (23a-25b). 34. A temperature management system (20) of claim 33, wherein - a pair of oppositely arranged, horizontally extending return air ducts (27a, 27b) configured to be disposed in the framework volume (104) and below said third pair of air ducts (25a, 25b) and at the bottom of the framework volume (104), said return air ducts (24a, 24b) being for extracting air from the storage volume (500) such that said air is conveyed towards the chilled air plant (22) via air conduits (53a, 53b). 35. A temperature management system (20) of claim 33 or claim 34, wherein the chilled air plant (22) is mounted externally with respect to the thermally isolated framework volume (104) containing the pairs of air ducts (23a-25b, 27a-2yb). 36. A temperature management system (20) of any of claims 33 - 35, wherein one chilled air plant (22) provides air for both air ducts of the pair of air ducts (23a-25b). 37- A temperature management system (20) of any of claims 33 - 36, wherein each air duct of the first and the second pair of oppositely arranged, horizontally extending air ducts (233-24!)) is provided with another air release device configured to release the air into the framework volume (104) in a horizontal direction, wherein said another air release device faces away from the corresponding first air release device. 38. A temperature management system (20) of any of claims 33 - 37, wherein the chilled air plant (22) further comprises an external dehumidifier.
